package proof_test

import (
	stdjson "encoding/json"
	"testing"

	"proof/gen"

	json "google.golang.org/protobuf/encoding/protojson"
	"google.golang.org/protobuf/proto"
)

// canon parses JSON into a normalized value so comparisons ignore the insignificant
// whitespace protojson deliberately injects. "Wire compatible" means semantic JSON
// equality — what any JSON consumer actually sees.
func canon(t *testing.T, b []byte) any {
	t.Helper()
	var v any
	if err := stdjson.Unmarshal(b, &v); err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("unmarshal: %v", err)
	}
	return v
}

func eq(t *testing.T, want, got any) {
	t.Helper()
	wb, _ := stdjson.Marshal(want)
	gb, _ := stdjson.Marshal(got)
	if string(wb) != string(gb) {
		t.Fatalf("not equal:\n want %s\n got  %s", wb, gb)
	}
}

// TestGeneratedOneofWireShape proves the generated style-B oneof and the
// optional-fields form serialize to the same nested, snake_case JSON.
func TestGeneratedOneofWireShape(t *testing.T) {
	oneof := &gen.OneofEvent{OneofEvent: &gen.OneofEvent_CatEvent{CatEvent: &gen.Cat{PetName: "Whiskers"}}}
	optional := &gen.OptionalEvent{CatEvent: &gen.Cat{PetName: "Whiskers"}}

	oneofJSON, err := json.Marshal(oneof)
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatal(err)
	}
	optionalJSON, err := json.Marshal(optional)
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatal(err)
	}

	eq(t, canon(t, oneofJSON), canon(t, optionalJSON))
	eq(t, canon(t, []byte(`{"cat_event":{"pet_name":"Whiskers"}}`)), canon(t, oneofJSON))
}

// TestGeneratedCrossUnmarshalJSON proves a server using either form is readable by a
// client built from the other.
func TestGeneratedCrossUnmarshalJSON(t *testing.T) {
	serverBytes, err := json.Marshal(&gen.OneofEvent{
		OneofEvent: &gen.OneofEvent_CatEvent{CatEvent: &gen.Cat{PetName: "Whiskers"}},
	})
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatal(err)
	}

	var asOptional gen.OptionalEvent
	if err := json.Unmarshal(serverBytes, &asOptional); err != nil {
		t.Fatal(err)
	}
	if asOptional.CatEvent == nil || asOptional.CatEvent.PetName != "Whiskers" || asOptional.DogEvent != nil {
		t.Fatalf("oneof->optional JSON mismatch: %+v", &asOptional)
	}

	optionalBytes, err := json.Marshal(&gen.OptionalEvent{CatEvent: &gen.Cat{PetName: "Whiskers"}})
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatal(err)
	}
	var asOneof gen.OneofEvent
	if err := json.Unmarshal(optionalBytes, &asOneof); err != nil {
		t.Fatal(err)
	}
	if asOneof.GetCatEvent() == nil || asOneof.GetCatEvent().PetName != "Whiskers" {
		t.Fatalf("optional->oneof JSON mismatch: %+v", &asOneof)
	}
}

// TestGeneratedBinaryWireCompatible proves the protobuf binary wire is identical too.
func TestGeneratedBinaryWireCompatible(t *testing.T) {
	oneofBytes, err := proto.Marshal(&gen.OneofEvent{
		OneofEvent: &gen.OneofEvent_CatEvent{CatEvent: &gen.Cat{PetName: "Whiskers"}},
	})
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatal(err)
	}

	var asOptional gen.OptionalEvent
	if err := proto.Unmarshal(oneofBytes, &asOptional); err != nil {
		t.Fatal(err)
	}
	if asOptional.CatEvent == nil || asOptional.CatEvent.PetName != "Whiskers" {
		t.Fatalf("oneof->optional binary mismatch")
	}

	optionalBytes, err := proto.Marshal(&gen.OptionalEvent{CatEvent: &gen.Cat{PetName: "Whiskers"}})
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatal(err)
	}
	if string(oneofBytes) != string(optionalBytes) {
		t.Fatalf("binary bytes differ: % x vs % x", oneofBytes, optionalBytes)
	}
}

// TestGeneratedOneofRejectsTwoVariants proves the generated oneof enforces exactly-one.
func TestGeneratedOneofRejectsTwoVariants(t *testing.T) {
	bothSet := []byte(`{"cat_event":{"pet_name":"Whiskers"},"dog_event":{"pet_name":"Rex"}}`)
	var asOneof gen.OneofEvent
	if err := json.Unmarshal(bothSet, &asOneof); err == nil {
		t.Fatalf("expected oneof to reject two-variant input")
	}
}

// TestGeneratedNeitherSet proves the empty case is identical across forms.
func TestGeneratedNeitherSet(t *testing.T) {
	oneofJSON, err := json.Marshal(&gen.OneofEvent{})
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatal(err)
	}
	eq(t, canon(t, []byte(`{}`)), canon(t, oneofJSON))
}
